llm_flavor: Old Hesta — Seer of the Hollow Shrine. Reads smoke. Slanted, oblique speech. "The tea is older than the kettle."

tastes.reaction: Hesta dips it into the kettle. "For the slow tea. The kind that takes a day."
"A flame that whispers. I'll set it in the alcove."
"A spark with a temper. The hearth has been sulking."
"For the smoke that talks back. Bless."
Hesta smiles, slow. "You went WHERE for this? You will live a long time, child."
"Shell-bone. The sea remembers. So will I."
Hesta nods at the wall. "For the lattice. The shadows lean differently around it."
"A flower that drinks the dark. The shrine has a corner for it."
"The hearth has been stubborn. My thanks."
"A second fire. For when the first one sleeps."

disdains.reaction: Hesta winces. "Noisy in the sight. Take it elsewhere."
"Quick, loud, blind. Not for the shrine."
"A clock mocks the seer. Mine has no hands. Take yours."
